item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for helping with spelling and grammar errors! Also, I was really worried if this article would make some believers feel uncomfortable.. Your opinion would help me to improve this article (if it did make people unhappy).

As for your explanation.. Yeah. In this article, the entity thought to be Satan was fictionalized as a mortal who later turned into a dragon. It is also possible that what they tried to fight against is another entity that was once human, or was a powerful type-green, or anything other than the true "God" in writer's universe. I hope such explanation works..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I took it upon myself to tidy up what spelling and grammar errors have been missed by others before me.

My opinion: I'm a faithful Sunni Muslim, and thus this starkly goes against my religious beliefs. However, that doesn't stop me from enjoying fictional stories involving such things, or even entertaining story ideas of that sort, since I simply handwave the "God" in each of them as not being the one that I believe in, but rather a vastly lesser entity (albeit one that is still so transcendent in comparison to us that it's impossible to notice the difference without meeting the real deal) that claims His identity for one reason or another. There's a whole host of sub-explanations that go into this, but they're irrelevant here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah.. That's a good point. I spent a large proportion of my effort on trying to make those references to well-known scps - I thought a 4000 article may work better with them, but yes, I need to potray a 4000 that can really impress the readers, or otherwise they will simply not be convinced by what's happening in the story.

Your feed back is important, thanks! If I rewrite this someday I will definitely look into this problem..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. This is kind of all over the place.

Not only do I have to agree with TL333s that this comes across as more prose than a clinical SCP article, but your characters here fall rather flat. SCP-4000 should ideally portray some kind of character. Even if they are not Satan himself, I'd imagine that the leader of a prehistoric/mythical army would be rather charismatic. Instead, this comes across as rather wooden, with choppy sentences that boil down to exposition. If this is 4000's attempt to win over the Foundation, they have utterly failed there.

That aside, you try to plot to weave several other bits of Foundation lore here into a consistent mythology and I don't think it gels as well as it might. I don't buy the Daevites locking themselves in a book as a means to escape the End, nor do I buy SCP-2000 as being created by the Clay. Nor SCP-682 as being a magical experiment.

On the whole, its an ambitious article, sure, but I feel in its ambition it sort of collapses in on itself, and crucial parts that are needed to carry the narrative are not there.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TBO as the author I'm not 100% satisfied with CN-343.. but now the whole story is completed and I'm really happy with that..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for liking!
I would really appreciate it if you could help with grammar.. I desperately need that kind of help :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly enjoy the concept of this SCP. The idea is fresh, new, and well executed, and I feel like it provides a very interesting take on Abrahamic Theology. When I get some time I'll post some grammar critiques in a collapsible, and if you'd like some help after the contest, I'd be more than happy to assist with a few of the grammar issues.

Overall a very enthusiastic +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rewritten from my favorite SCP-CN…and it's just wonderful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is part of the article that I liked and your reasoning is very astute. A 4000 article should be about both the history of the world and of the foundation, and I think 682 is obviously relevant to the latter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see what you mean. I have edited the description so the sentences are in proper order now. Thanks for pointing out…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article contains one of my biggest pet peeves. Not only do you not use the first sentence of the description to tell us what 4000 is, you don't even use it to tell us what the -A is. Start with the entity chained up on 4000-A, then tell us about -A.

The description is otherwise too prosaic for an official document, and that purple text drew me out of the whole thing more or less immediately. It's silly and I don't know why it's there. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am considering to blackbox the scp number after the contest. :D but now, I suppose it is 4000 so it maybe able to explain some of the long-existed mysteries or refer to some well-known skips..
Thanks for telling!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.. I cannot do this without your advice. Thanks again for that..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely ambitious; I remember reviewing this. Not quite to my taste, so a novote from me here, but I'm happy to see it in the positives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you enjoy it!

Since I'm a bit worried about my English..thanks for letting me know people can understand what's going on in this article._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At about the point where I finished the Black Events, I noticed a pattern. People suddenly becoming happy, terminal illnesses being cured at random, volcanoes erupting and spitting out happy things… it made me think back to the title: “You Shall Be Free.” And I really like seeing connections like those.

This is totally worth the long read.

As a straightforward… know-er of the english language (please don’t quote me on that), I can’t help but notice the off-seeming sentence structure and phrasing- which is, albeit, more concentrated in the addendums and transcripts than the entire work itself- but that doesn’t detract from its quality as an SCP at all, and it’s still very clear what’s going on. I think this is a solid 4000 contestant.

Upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this, it put everything into detail very well and actually allowed me to imagine it properly, you have my +1.

I really didn't like the 682 reference, I don't feel as though it fits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really appreciate your help! Thanks!

The two sentences are edited according to your advice. (The first sentence is deleted because it is not important._.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

I took the opportunity presented by your above comment to edit all grammatical mistakes I saw, and recommend alternative word choices on two occasions. Edits include the following:

  • The use of the word "illegible" used multiple times to describe sounds; changed to "unintelligible", as illegible is specific to the act of reading.
  • The completion of incomplete ellipses.
  • The replacement of "humans nowadays" in O5 Council documentation to "present-day humans".
  • Various and minor disagreements, e.g. "is/are", "has/had/have been", etc.

I couldn't however help much with the following sentences, I'm not sure what you are getting at:

A sufficient flow of pilgrim must be ensured to keep Akiva radiation at baseline.

The Senior Research Team is to reside on-site permanently due to the possible unexpected effect should they leave.

I hope this helps, feel free to revert if not.

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
The article is a rewrite of SCP-CN-343 (which is also written by me). I know my English is flawed but this is all I can do.. Thank you for reading such a long article.

Image source
– hide block

Dragon Icon:
Made by myself. Subject to CC-BY-SA 3.0

Image of -1:
http://source.pixite.co/jamesmgrote/digital-landscapes?photo=My7Y28j8dGAp5ysvF
James Grote, Public Domain

Image of bathmetry:
http://www.ngdc.noaa.gov/mgg/image/2minrelief.html , NOAA, Public Domain

Some settings about the Smoke comes from ORIA Memorandum 001, which I tried to integrate into the world view of this SCP. If the author does not like what is going on with their work here I would happily remove the part regarding to the Smoke.

Easters eggs and thoughts (spoilers inside)
– hide block

File 4000/031 - Yes, this is the war in heavens as described in Bible. The deputy resembles the "beast" to whom the dragon gave his authority. And they somehow "healed from fatal wound" and "gave people markings".

File 4000/032 matches the Gun-Yu Legend.

The "primitive electricity generators for thaumaturgic purposes" may or may not be Baghdad Battery._.

And the Azores / City of Orders? Its location somehow coincides with Plato's description of Atlantis, and I am surprised to find out that it also coincides with that of Mount Buzhou in Chinese legends. To be short, according to legends, it was a mountain that supported the heavens, and when a heavenly war broke out, the mountain was smashed. As a result, skys fell and and earth cracked, causing a great flood.

The Unseen is originally an Islamic theological concept (الغيب, secrets known only to Allāh, sometimes considered to be related with magic and future-telling), but in this skip, the concept may have different meanings.

I always wonder what happened in those very ancient times. According to Book of Shang(尚书) and Book of History(史记)(both are history recorded by court officials in ancient China), ancient emperors before the flood (~4000 years ago) lived very, very long life(>100). Legends say they can command magical creatures. Then I figure out that human, as described in Bible, can live 120 years before the flood. When I brainstormed this skip I thought about the legends. What if they are true? What if ancient civillizations and prehistoric civillizations are much more advanced than us?

Though flood myth exists among various cultures, the time that a flood occurred is inconsistent. At first I cannot make a proper time setting, and then I come up with the idea that end of the world has happened many times before. The Qur'an also mentioned that there are "generations and generations We destroyed before you", and, here it is.

Many thanks to:
MottHery
Drblackbox
Zyn Special thanks to Zyn for their careful review and long piece of crit!
Hueyacuetzpali
scpcrnp for proofreading and spotting so many errors

If any author not want their scps be crosslinked in this article, please contact me and I will be happy to remove it.
